verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचस एवमनुभूयेदं नारायणविनिर्मितम् ।वैभवं योगमायायास्तमेव शरणं ययौ ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācasa evam anubhūyedaṁnārāyaṇa-vinirmitamvaibhavaṁ yoga-māyāyāstam eva śaraṇaṁ yayau
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: The Supreme Lord Nārāyaṇa had arranged this opulent display of His bewildering potency. Mārkaṇḍeya Ṛṣi, having experienced it, took shelter of the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीमार्कण्डेय उवाचप्रपन्नोऽस्म्यङ्‍‍घ्रिमूलं ते प्रपन्नाभयदं हरे ।यन्माययापि विबुधा मुह्यन्ति ज्ञानकाशया ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-mārkaṇḍeya uvācaprapanno ’smy aṅghri-mūlaṁ teprapannābhaya-daṁ hareyan-māyayāpi vibudhāmuhyanti jñāna-kāśayā
verses.text: Śrī Mārkaṇḍeya said: O Lord Hari, I take shelter of the soles of Your lotus feet, which bestow fearlessness upon all who surrender to them. Even the great demigods are bewildered by Your illusory energy, which appears to them in the guise of knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचतमेवं निभृतात्मानं वृषेण दिवि पर्यटन् ।रुद्राण्या भगवान् रुद्रो ददर्श स्वगणैर्वृत: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācatam evaṁ nibhṛtātmānaṁvṛṣeṇa divi paryaṭanrudrāṇyā bhagavān rudrodadarśa sva-gaṇair vṛtaḥ
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Lord Rudra, traveling in the sky on his bull and accompanied by his consort, Rudrāṇī, as well as his personal associates, observed Mārkaṇḍeya in trance.

verses.sanskrit: अथोमा तमृषिं वीक्ष्य गिरिशं समभाषत ।पश्येमं भगवन् विप्रं निभृतात्मेन्द्रियाशयम् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: athomā tam ṛṣiṁ vīkṣyagiriśaṁ samabhāṣatapaśyemaṁ bhagavan vipraṁnibhṛtātmendriyāśayam
verses.text: Goddess Umā, seeing the sage, addressed Lord Giriśa: My lord, just see this learned brāhmaṇa, his body, mind and senses motionless in trance.

verses.sanskrit: निभृतोदझषव्रातो वातापाये यथार्णव: ।कुर्वस्य तपस: साक्षात् संसिद्धिं सिद्धिदो भवान् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: nibhṛtoda-jhaṣa-vrātovātāpāye yathārṇavaḥkurv asya tapasaḥ sākṣātsaṁsiddhiṁ siddhi-do bhavān
verses.text: He is as calm as the waters of the ocean when the wind has ceased and the fish remain still. Therefore, my lord, since you bestow perfection on the performers of austerity, please award this sage the perfection that is obviously due him.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचनैवेच्छत्याशिष: क्‍वापि ब्रह्मर्षिर्मोक्षमप्युत ।भक्तिं परां भगवति लब्धवान् पुरुषेऽव्यये ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācanaivecchaty āśiṣaḥ kvāpibrahmarṣir mokṣam apy utabhaktiṁ parāṁ bhagavatilabdhavān puruṣe ’vyaye
verses.text: Lord Śiva replied: Surely this saintly brāhmaṇa does not desire any benediction, not even liberation itself, for he has attained pure devotional service unto the inexhaustible Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: अथापि संवदिष्यामो भवान्येतेन साधुना ।अयं हि परमो लाभो नृणां साधुसमागम: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: athāpi saṁvadiṣyāmobhavāny etena sādhunāayaṁ hi paramo lābhonṛṇāṁ sādhu-samāgamaḥ
verses.text: Still, my dear Bhavānī, let us talk with this saintly personality. After all, association with saintly devotees is man’s highest achievement.

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचइत्युक्त्वा तमुपेयाय भगवान् स सतां गति: ।ईशान: सर्वविद्यानामीश्वर: सर्वदेहिनाम् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācaity uktvā tam upeyāyabhagavān sa satāṁ gatiḥīśānaḥ sarva-vidyānāmīśvaraḥ sarva-dehinām
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Having spoken thus, Lord Śaṅkara — the shelter of pure souls, master of all spiritual sciences and controller of all embodied living beings — approached the sage.

verses.sanskrit: तयोरागमनं साक्षादीशयोर्जगदात्मनो: ।न वेद रुद्धधीवृत्तिरात्मानं विश्वमेव च ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tayor āgamanaṁ sākṣādīśayor jagad-ātmanoḥna veda ruddha-dhī-vṛttirātmānaṁ viśvam eva ca
verses.text: Because Mārkaṇḍeya’s material mind had stopped functioning, the sage failed to notice that Lord Śiva and his wife, the controllers of the universe, had personally come to see him. Mārkaṇḍeya was so absorbed in meditation that he was unaware of either himself or the external world.

verses.sanskrit: भगवांस्तदभिज्ञाय गिरिशो योगमायया ।आविशत्तद्गुहाकाशं वायुश्छिद्रमिवेश्वर: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: bhagavāṁs tad abhijñāyagiriśo yoga-māyayāāviśat tad-guhākāśaṁvāyuś chidram iveśvaraḥ
verses.text: Understanding the situation very well, the powerful Lord Śiva employed his mystic power to enter within the sky of Mārkaṇḍeya’s heart, just as the wind passes through an opening.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मन्यपि शिवं प्राप्तं तडित्पिङ्गजटाधरम् ।त्र्यक्षं दशभुजं प्रांशुमुद्यन्तमिव भास्करम् ॥ ११ ॥व्याघ्रचर्माम्बरं शूलधनुरिष्वसिचर्मभि: ।अक्षमालाडमरुककपालं परशुं सह ॥ १२ ॥बिभ्राणं सहसा भातं विचक्ष्य हृदि विस्मित: ।किमिदं कुत एवेति समाधेर्विरतो मुनि: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: ātmany api śivaṁ prāptaṁtaḍit-piṅga-jaṭā-dharamtry-akṣaṁ daśa-bhujaṁ prāṁśumudyantam iva bhāskaram
verses.text: Śrī Mārkaṇḍeya saw Lord Śiva suddenly appear within his heart. Lord Śiva’s golden hair resembled lightning, and he had three eyes, ten arms and a tall body that shone like the rising sun. He wore a tiger skin, and he carried a trident, a bow, arrows, a sword and a shield, along with prayer beads, a ḍamaru drum, a skull and an ax. Astonished, the sage came out of his trance and thought, “Who is this, and where has he come from?”

verses.sanskrit: नेत्रे उन्मील्य दद‍ृशे सगणं सोमयागतम् ।रुद्रं त्रिलोकैकगुरुं ननाम शिरसा मुनि: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: netre unmīlya dadṛśesa-gaṇaṁ somayāgatamrudraṁ tri-lokaika-guruṁnanāma śirasā muniḥ
verses.text: Opening his eyes, the sage saw Lord Rudra, the spiritual master of the three worlds, together with Umā and Rudra’s followers. Mārkaṇḍeya then offered his respectful obeisances by bowing his head.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मै सपर्यां व्यदधात् सगणाय सहोमया ।स्वागतासनपाद्यार्घ्यगन्धस्रग्धूपदीपकै: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmai saparyāṁ vyadadhātsa-gaṇāya sahomayāsvāgatāsana-pādyārghya-gandha-srag-dhūpa-dīpakaiḥ
verses.text: Mārkaṇḍeya worshiped Lord Śiva, along with Umā and Śiva’s associates, by offering them words of welcome, sitting places, water for washing their feet, scented drinking water, fragrant oils, flower garlands and ārati lamps.

verses.sanskrit: आह त्वात्मानुभावेन पूर्णकामस्य ते विभो ।करवाम किमीशान येनेदं निर्वृतं जगत् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: āha tv ātmānubhāvenapūrṇa-kāmasya te vibhokaravāma kim īśānayenedaṁ nirvṛtaṁ jagat
verses.text: Mārkaṇḍeya said: O mighty lord, what can I possibly do for you, who are fully satisfied by your own ecstasy? Indeed, by your mercy you satisfy this entire world.

verses.sanskrit: नम: शिवाय शान्ताय सत्त्वाय प्रमृडाय च ।रजोजुषेऽथ घोराय नमस्तुभ्यं तमोजुषे ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: namaḥ śivāya śāntāyasattvāya pramṛḍāya carajo-juṣe ’tha ghorāyanamas tubhyaṁ tamo-juṣe
verses.text: Again and again I offer my obeisances unto you, O all-auspicious transcendental personality. As the lord of goodness you give pleasure, in contact with the mode of passion you appear most fearful, and you also associate with the mode of ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचएवं स्तुत: स भगवानादिदेव: सतां गति: ।परितुष्ट: प्रसन्नात्मा प्रहसंस्तमभाषत ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācaevaṁ stutaḥ sa bhagavānādi-devaḥ satāṁ gatiḥparituṣṭaḥ prasannātmāprahasaṁs tam abhāṣata
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Lord Śiva, the foremost demigod and the shelter of the saintly devotees, was satisfied by Mārkaṇḍeya’s praise. Pleased, he smiled and addressed the sage.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचवरं वृणीष्व न: कामं वरदेशा वयं त्रय: ।अमोघं दर्शनं येषां मर्त्यो यद् विन्दतेऽमृतम् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācavaraṁ vṛṇīṣva naḥ kāmaṁvara-deśā vayaṁ trayaḥamoghaṁ darśanaṁ yeṣāṁmartyo yad vindate ’mṛtam
verses.text: Lord Śiva said: Please ask me for some benediction, since among all givers of benedictions, we three — Brahmā, Viṣṇu and I — are the best. Seeing us never goes in vain, because simply by seeing us a mortal achieves immortality.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणा: साधव: शान्ता नि:सङ्गा भूतवत्सला: ।एकान्तभक्ता अस्मासु निर्वैरा: समदर्शिन: ॥ २० ॥सलोका लोकपालास्तान् वन्दन्त्यर्चन्त्युपासते ।अहं च भगवान् ब्रह्मा स्वयं च हरिरीश्वर: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: brāhmaṇāḥ sādhavaḥ śāntāniḥsaṅgā bhūta-vatsalāḥekānta-bhaktā asmāsunirvairāḥ sama-darśinaḥ
verses.text: The inhabitants and ruling demigods of all planets, along with Lord Brahmā, the Supreme Lord Hari and I, glorify, worship and assist those brāhmaṇas who are saintly, always peaceful, free of material attachment, compassionate to all living beings, purely devoted to us, devoid of hatred and endowed with equal vision.

verses.sanskrit: न ते मय्यच्युतेऽजे च भिदामण्वपि चक्षते ।नात्मनश्च जनस्यापि तद् युष्मान् वयमीमहि ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: na te mayy acyute ’je cabhidām aṇv api cakṣatenātmanaś ca janasyāpitad yuṣmān vayam īmahi
verses.text: These devotees do not differentiate between Lord Viṣṇu, Lord Brahmā and me, nor do they differentiate between themselves and other living beings. Therefore, because you are this kind of saintly devotee, we worship you.

verses.sanskrit: न ह्यम्मयानि तीर्थानि न देवाश्चेतनोज्झिता: ।ते पुनन्त्युरुकालेन यूयं दर्शनमात्रत: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: na hy am-mayāni tīrthānina devāś cetanojjhitāḥte punanty uru-kālenayūyaṁ darśana-mātrataḥ
verses.text: Mere bodies of water do not constitute holy places, nor are lifeless statues of the demigods actual worshipable deities. Because external vision fails to appreciate the higher essence of the holy rivers and the demigods, these purify only after a considerable time. But devotees like you purify immediately, just by being seen.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणेभ्यो नमस्यामो येऽस्मद्रूपं त्रयीमयम् ।बिभ्रत्यात्मसमाधानतप:स्वाध्यायसंयमै: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: brāhmaṇebhyo namasyāmoye ’smad-rūpaṁ trayī-mayambibhraty ātma-samādhāna-tapaḥ-svādhyāya-saṁyamaiḥ
verses.text: By meditating upon the Supreme Soul, performing austerities, engaging in Vedic study and following regulative principles, the brāhmaṇas sustain within themselves the three Vedas, which are nondifferent from Lord Viṣṇu, Lord Brahmā and me. Therefore I offer my obeisances unto the brāhmaṇas.

verses.sanskrit: श्रवणाद् दर्शनाद् वापि महापातकिनोऽपि व: ।शुध्येरन्नन्त्यजाश्चापि किमु सम्भाषणादिभि: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śravaṇād darśanād vāpimahā-pātakino ’pi vaḥśudhyerann antya-jāś cāpikim u sambhāṣaṇādibhiḥ
verses.text: Even the worst sinners and social outcastes are purified just by hearing about or seeing personalities like you. Imagine, then, how purified they become by directly speaking with you.

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचइति चन्द्रललामस्य धर्मगुह्योपबृंहितम् ।वचोऽमृतायनमृषिर्नातृप्यत् कर्णयो: पिबन् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācaiti candra-lalāmasyadharma-gahyopabṛṁhitamvaco ’mṛtāyanam ṛṣirnātṛpyat karṇayoḥ piban
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Drinking with his ears Lord Śiva’s nectarean words, full of the confidential essence of religion, Mārkaṇḍeya Ṛṣi could not be satiated.

verses.sanskrit: स चिरं मायया विष्णोर्भ्रामित: कर्शितो भृशम् ।शिववागमृतध्वस्तक्लेशपुञ्जस्तमब्रवीत् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa ciraṁ māyayā viṣṇorbhrāmitaḥ karśito bhṛśamśiva-vāg-amṛta-dhvasta-kleśa-puñjas tam abravīt
verses.text: Mārkaṇḍeya, having been forced by Lord Viṣṇu’s illusory energy to wander about for a long time in the water of dissolution, had become extremely exhausted. But Lord Śiva’s words of nectar vanquished his accumulated suffering. Thus he addressed Lord Śiva.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीमार्कण्डेय उवाचअहो ईश्वरलीलेयं दुर्विभाव्या शरीरिणाम् ।यन्नमन्तीशितव्यानि स्तुवन्ति जगदीश्वरा: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-mārkaṇḍeya uvācaaho īśvara-līleyaṁdurvibhāvyā śarīriṇāmyan namantīśitavyānistuvanti jagad-īśvarāḥ
verses.text: Śrī Mārkaṇḍeya said: It is indeed most difficult for embodied souls to understand the pastimes of the universal controllers, for such lords bow down to and offer praise to the very living beings they rule.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मं ग्राहयितुं प्राय: प्रवक्तारश्च देहिनाम् ।आचरन्त्यनुमोदन्ते क्रियमाणं स्तुवन्ति च ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dharmaṁ grāhayituṁ prāyaḥpravaktāraś ca dehināmācaranty anumodantekriyamāṇaṁ stuvanti ca
verses.text: Generally it is to induce embodied souls to accept religious principles that the authorized teachers of religion exhibit ideal behavior while encouraging and praising the proper behavior of others.

verses.sanskrit: नैतावता भगवत: स्वमायामयवृत्तिभि: ।न दुष्येतानुभावस्तैर्मायिन: कुहकं यथा ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: naitāvatā bhagavataḥsva-māyā-maya-vṛttibhiḥna duṣyetānubhāvas tairmāyinaḥ kuhakaṁ yathā
verses.text: This apparent humility is simply a show of mercy. Such behavior of the Supreme Lord and His personal associates, which the Lord effects by His own bewildering potency, does not spoil His power any more than a magician’s powers are diminished by his exhibition of tricks.

verses.sanskrit: सृष्ट्वेदं मनसा विश्वमात्मनानुप्रविश्य य: ।गुणै: कुर्वद्भ‍िराभाति कर्तेव स्वप्नद‍ृग् यथा ॥ ३१ ॥तस्मै नमो भगवते त्रिगुणाय गुणात्मने ।केवलायाद्वितीयाय गुरवे ब्रह्ममूर्तये ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sṛṣṭvedaṁ manasā viśvamātmanānupraviśya yaḥguṇaiḥ kurvadbhir ābhātikarteva svapna-dṛg yathā
verses.text: I offer my obeisances to that Supreme Personality of Godhead, who has created this entire universe simply by His desire and then entered into it as the Supersoul. By making the modes of nature act, He seems to be the direct creator of this world, just as a dreamer seems to be acting within his dream. He is the owner and ultimate controller of the three modes of nature, yet He remains alone and pure, without any equal. He is the supreme spiritual master of all, the original personal form of the Absolute Truth.

verses.sanskrit: कं वृणे नु परं भूमन् वरं त्वद् वरदर्शनात् ।यद्दर्शनात् पूर्णकाम: सत्यकाम: पुमान् भवेत् ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaṁ vṛṇe nu paraṁ bhūmanvaraṁ tvad vara-darśanātyad-darśanāt pūrṇa-kāmaḥsatya-kāmaḥ pumān bhavet
verses.text: O all-pervading lord, since I have received the benediction of seeing you, what other benediction can I ask for? Simply by seeing you, a person fulfills all his desires and can achieve anything imaginable.

verses.sanskrit: वरमेकं वृणेऽथापि पूर्णात् कामाभिवर्षणात् ।भगवत्यच्युतां भक्तिं तत्परेषु तथा त्वयि ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: varam ekaṁ vṛṇe ’thāpipūrṇāt kāmābhivarṣaṇātbhagavaty acyutāṁ bhaktiṁtat-pareṣu tathā tvayi
verses.text: But I do request one benediction from you, who are full of all perfection and able to shower down the fulfillment of all desires. I ask to have unfailing devotion for the Supreme Personality of Godhead and for His dedicated devotees, especially you.

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचइत्यर्चितोऽभिष्टुतश्च मुनिना सूक्तया गिरा ।तमाह भगवाञ्छर्व: शर्वया चाभिनन्दित: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācaity arcito ’bhiṣṭutaś camuninā sūktayā girātam āha bhagavāñ charvaḥśarvayā cābhinanditaḥ
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Thus worshiped and glorified by the eloquent statements of the sage Mārkaṇḍeya, Lord Śarva [Śiva], encouraged by his consort, replied to him as follows.

verses.sanskrit: कामो महर्षे सर्वोऽयं भक्तिमांस्त्वमधोक्षजे ।आकल्पान्ताद् यश: पुण्यमजरामरता तथा ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: kāmo maharṣe sarvo ’yaṁbhaktimāṁs tvam adhokṣajeā-kalpāntād yaśaḥ puṇyamajarāmaratā tathā
verses.text: O great sage, because you are devoted to Lord Adhokṣaja, all your desires will be fulfilled. Until the very end of this creation cycle, you will enjoy pious fame and freedom from old age and death.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानं त्रैकालिकं ब्रह्मन् विज्ञानं च विरक्तिमत् ।ब्रह्मवर्चस्विनो भूयात् पुराणाचार्यतास्तु ते ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: jñānaṁ trai-kālikaṁ brahmanvijñānaṁ ca viraktimatbrahma-varcasvino bhūyātpurāṇācāryatāstu te
verses.text: O brāhmaṇa, may you have perfect knowledge of past, present and future, along with transcendental realization of the Supreme, enriched by renunciation. You have the brilliance of an ideal brāhmaṇa, and thus may you achieve the post of spiritual master of the Purāṇas.

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचएवं वरान् स मुनये दत्त्वागात् त्र्यक्ष ईश्वर: ।देव्यै तत्कर्म कथयन्ननुभूतं पुरामुना ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācaevaṁ varān sa munayedattvāgāt try-akṣa īśvaraḥdevyai tat-karma kathayannanubhūtaṁ purāmunā
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Having thus granted Mārkaṇḍeya Ṛṣi benedictions, Lord Śiva went on his way, continuing to describe to goddess Devī the accomplishments of the sage and the direct exhibition of the Lord’s illusory power that he had experienced.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽप्यवाप्तमहायोगमहिमा भार्गवोत्तम: ।विचरत्यधुनाप्यद्धा हरावेकान्ततां गत: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’py avāpta-mahā-yoga-mahimā bhārgavottamaḥvicaraty adhunāpy addhāharāv ekāntatāṁ gataḥ
verses.text: Mārkaṇḍeya Ṛṣi, the best of the descendants of Bhṛgu, is glorious because of his achievement of perfection in mystic yoga. Even today he travels about this world, fully absorbed in unalloyed devotion for the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: अनुवर्णितमेतत्ते मार्कण्डेयस्य धीमत: ।अनुभूतं भगवतो मायावैभवमद्भ‍ुतम् ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: anuvarṇitam etat temārkaṇḍeyasya dhīmataḥanubhūtaṁ bhagavatomāyā-vaibhavam adbhutam
verses.text: I have thus narrated to you the activities of the highly intelligent sage Mārkaṇḍeya, especially how he experienced the amazing power of the Supreme Lord’s illusory energy.

verses.sanskrit: एतत् केचिदविद्वांसो मायासंसृतिरात्मन: ।अनाद्यावर्तितं नृणां कादाचित्कं प्रचक्षते ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: etat kecid avidvāṁsomāyā-saṁsṛtir ātmanaḥanādy-āvartitaṁ nṝṇāṁkādācitkaṁ pracakṣate
verses.text: Although this event was unique and unprecedented, some unintelligent persons compare it to the cycle of illusory material existence the Supreme Lord has created for the conditioned souls — an endless cycle that has been continuing since time immemorial.

verses.sanskrit: य एवमेतद् भृगुवर्य वर्णितंरथाङ्गपाणेरनुभावभावितम् ।संश्रावयेत् संश‍ृणुयादु तावुभौतयोर्न कर्माशयसंसृतिर्भवेत् ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: ya evam etad bhṛgu-varya varṇitaṁrathāṅga-pāṇer anubhāva-bhāvitamsaṁśrāvayet saṁśṛṇuyād u tāv ubhautayor na karmāśaya-saṁsṛtir bhavet
verses.text: O best of the Bhṛgus, this account concerning Mārkaṇḍeya Ṛṣi conveys the transcendental potency of the Supreme Lord. Anyone who properly narrates or hears it will never again undergo material existence, which is based on the desire to perform fruitive activities.
